data.title: STMicroelectronics launches AI microcontrollers with machine learning capability
data.description:
STMicroelectronics (NYSE:STM) is ready to launch its new series of microcontrollers with machine learning capabilities in high volumes.

The Switzerland-based company said the STM32N6 microcontroller, or MCU, series is its most powerful to date, and the first to embed its proprietary neural processing unit, or NPU, the Neural-ART Accelerator. The system delivers 600 times more machine-learning performance than a high-end STM32 MCU today, according to the company.

New machine-learning capabilities make it possible to run computer vision, audio processing, sound analysis and more consumer and industrial applications at the edge. Until now, such capabilities were beyond small embedded systems, the company added.

The system is supported by the company's Edge AI Suite, which offers software tools for developing edge machine-learning applications.

STMicroelectronics noted that STM32N6 has been available to selected customers since October last year and is now ready to be offered in high volumes.

"It will utilize a new release of our unique AI software ecosystem package. This marks the beginning of a long journey of AI hardware-accelerated STM32, which will enable innovations in applications and products in ways not possible with any other embedded processing solution," said Remi El-Ouazzane, President, Microcontrollers, Digital ICs and RF Products Group, or MDRF, at STMicroelectronics.

LG Electronics has been using STM32N6, according to the company.

Yehan Ahn, task leader, R&D, LG Electronics CTO division, said that the inference speed exceeded their expectations and STMicroelectronics' software tools allows them to seamlessly integrate their AI models into the MCU.

Generative AI large language models, or LLMs, such as OpenAI's ChatGPT or Google's Gemini are trained and run from large data centers. However, edge AI uses similar principles but less computing power for a limited localized use, such as in a facility or wearable device. This does not potentially require large amounts of data to be sent to data centers to be processed and then returned, thus saving time and electric energy.

STMicroelectronics noted that Meta-bounds — a China-based innovator in the consumer-level Augmented Reality, or AR, glasses industry — has also used STM32N6.

"The embedded Neural-ART Accelerator, enhanced camera interfaces and dedicated Image Signal Processor (ISP), allow us to deliver compelling features in an ultra-lightweight and compact form factor, enhancing the user experience without compromising battery life," said Zhou Xing, Founding Partner of Meta-bounds.

STMicroelectronics' stock was down about 1% on Tuesday.

data.title: Oscar Health stock dips amid Jefferies underperform rating
data.description:
Shares of Oscar Health (NYSE:OSCR) slid 3% after Jefferies initiated coverage of the healthcare insurer with an underperform rating, citing industry headwinds.

The investment bank said it sees healthcare insurance exchange market growth, which has been over-stimulated by enhanced subsidies that are set to expire at the end of 2025 and lax disclosure enforcement, as "beginning to unwind."

It noted that a recent crackdown on broker fraud by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services “poses risk to not only near-term membership estimates but margin, too, as plans likely underappreciated, and therefore mispriced, the number of non-utilizers that will be churned in 2025.” It added that it sees industry enrollment declining 28% from 2024 to 2027.

While praising the quality of Oscar's (OSCR) management, Jefferies also questioned whether they would be overpowered by sector headwinds.

"With Oscar's (OSCR) overexposure to subsidy-sensitive states, even this strong management is unlikely to fully mitigate those headwinds with ICHRA and buybacks," the bank added.

The investment bank set its price target for the stock at $12.

data.title: CoreCivic upgraded at Wedbush on 'higher potential ICE detention utilization'
data.description:
A manager of private detention centers, CoreCivic (NYSE:CXW), was rising after Wedbush Securities upgraded the stock on "higher potential ICE detention utilization".

The stock was trading +4.15% at $22.84 Tuesday, late morning.

Wedbush had downgraded CXW earlier this year on the loss of South Texas contract, citing it represented a notable portion of the company's run-rate EBITDA.

"Given recent commentary coming from the Trump administration's transition team, we now believe that the need for incremental ICE beds could be even higher than previously expected, and that this need for beds could result in a reactivation of the lost South Texas contract," said the financial services firm.

A potential uptick in EBITDA could result in an acceleration in the share repurchase activity, which has been on hold following the loss of the contract, according to the research note.

The stock was upgraded to Outperform from Neutral, and the price target was raised to $30 from $19.

The rating aligns with the average sell-side analyst rating of Buy. Seeking Alpha authors as well as the Quant Rating system grades CXW as Hold.

"CXW is at the precipice of an interesting juncture which could see its operating market well-positioned to capture a series of structural tailwinds brought on by US government policy," said SA contributor Zach Bristow.

"The investment case for CXW is limited. And in my opinion, after crunching the numbers and potential distribution of outcomes, even a rapid uptick in sales revenue from these policies might not be enough to move the needle above investors could expect to receive elsewhere," noted the author.

data.title: James Hardie Industries rated Buy in new coverage at Truist
data.description:
James Hardie Industries (NYSE:JHX) on Tuesday was rated as a Buy in new coverage by analysts at financial-services firm Truist. They said shares of the maker of building materials are poised to gain value as earnings expand.

“James Hardie’s (JHX) core product is the Hardie Plank fiber cement siding that we believe provides a better alternative to wood and vinyl residential siding,” Keith Hughes, analyst at Truist, said in a December 10 report. “The product has far greater durability and much better aesthetics than these options, in our view. The company has developed a ‘pull strategy’ with significant consumer brand name recognition.”

Truist forecasts James Hardie’s (JHX) fiber cement siding business will be strong in terms of volume and pricing. The company also has healthy operating margins, and can expand its multiples by listing stock instead of American depositary receipts.

Truist set a price target of $45 a share on James Hardie’s (JHX) stock, based on an estimated enterprise value that’s 18 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization for 2025.

data.title: S&P 500 wanders as Alphabet climbs, Oracle sells off; Nasdaq hits record intraday high
data.description:
Major U.S. stock averages were seeking firm direction Tuesday, with Big Tech back in the spotlight as communication services stocks climbed while Oracle (ORCL) suffered following its quarterly update.

Stocks were coming off a negative session on Monday, after notching a three-week win streak. A cautious tone has been setting in ahead of consumer and wholesale inflation reports due this week.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.1% after scaling a fresh intraday record high of 19,887.08 points. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was flat after slight gains. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) edged up +0.1% after moving lower.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 3 basis points to 4.23%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was also up 3 basis points to 4.16%.

The S&P 500's (SP500) Communication Services sector +2%, pacing gains in four other sectors, bolstered by Alphabet. Class A (GOOGL) and class C (GOOG) shares of the tech titan rose more than +4%, moving up a day after the company revealed a powerful quantum computing chip.

But Oracle (ORCL) on the index sank -7.3% Tuesday after the cloud software company disappointed investors with fiscal second-quarter results that slightly missed expectations.

Traders will receive the consumer price inflation for November on Wednesday, ahead of next week's Federal Reserve monetary policy committee meeting, the last of the year. The core consumer price index is expected to come in at 0.3% M/M, and 3.3% Y/Y.

The headline number is forecast to come in at 0.2% on a monthly basis, and 2.7% annually.

"That release (CPI) is going to be a crucial one, as it’s probably the last big piece of the jigsaw ahead of the Fed’s policy decision next week," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

In other stock movers, developer data platform MongoDB (MDB) -13% despite delivering a top- and bottom-line beat and lifting its annual revenue and adjusted profit guidance

In Tuesday's economic calendar, Q3 labor costs were revised down, while the productivity number stayed at 2.2%.

data.title: UBS drops Sell rating on Victoria's Secret as Q3 shows inflection in comp sales
data.description:
Victoria’s Secret's (NYSE:VSCO) recent quarterly report gives UBS’s Mauricio Serna confidence in the CEO’s turnaround plan and ability to regain relevance among millennials, encouraging the analyst to drop his Sell rating on the stock for a Neutral outlook.

“We have more conviction that [Victoria’s Secret] CEO Hillary Super's turnaround plan can help the company drive sustainable growth,” Serna said, adding that he also believes market share pressures are subsiding given the company’s North American sales grew in Q3, outperforming the intimates industry’s low-single-digit percentage rate.

“We have a more bullish macro view and this robust consumer spending environment should benefit mall-based retailers like VSCO,” Serna said.

Driven by strong sales momentum in Q3 that continued through Black Friday and Cyber Monday thanks to giftable product assortments that resonated with customers, Victoria’s Secret (VSCO) raised their FY24 sales and income forecast and reported a narrower-than-expected loss for Q3. More importantly, comparable sales for Q3 inflected to positive 3% from negative 3% in Q2, a key component to UBS’s upgrade along with a 7% year-over-year increase in net sales.

“Our upside case in new CEO Hillary Super’s focus on product, brands’ differentiation, and innovation drive sustained mid-single-digit sales growth into FY25, resulting in significant operating margin recapture and upward earnings revisions over the course of FY25,” Serna said.

Together with an upgrade, Serna more than doubled his price target for Victoria’s Secret (VSCO) to $47.

VSCO has surrendered early gains tied to the upgrade and are now trading ~4% lower after rejecting resistance at the 2 ½ year high of $48.70.

data.title: Citi sees investment banking fees rising as much as 30% Y/Y in Q4
data.description:
Citigroup's (NYSE:C) investment banking fees are expected to climb 25%-30% Y/Y in Q4, and market revenue is expected to rise by "high-teens" percentage, Chief Financial Officer Mark Mason said Tuesday at a financial industry conference.

Gains continue in equity capital markets, debt capital markets and M&A.

It's "looking to be very solid there as we continue to gain share there," he said.

NII ex-markets likely to be flat Q/Q, and that's "still the case."

Cost of credit should be about $2.6B for Q4, Mason added.

"I feel very good about the (revenue) guidance we've given for 2024 — the $80B-$81B; we're likely to be at the high end of that range," he said.

There's no change related to expense guidance.

"The global economy has proven to be quite resilient," he said, with the U.S. particularly "strong and resilient."

"The general sentiment is one towards pro-growth, as we go into 2025, from a corporate point of view," he said. Meanwhile, "the consumer has held up nicely."

Holiday spending has increased mid-single digits Y/Y.

Citi (NYSE:C) stock rose 1.1% in Tuesday late-morning trading.

data.title: Boeing's plane deliveries decline to 13 in November as strike ends
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) in November delivered 13 commercial jets as the aviation giant worked to resume production following a seven-week strike by machinists. A year earlier, the company delivered 56 planes to customers including airlines.

Boeing (BA) has sought to increase the output of its best-selling 737 Max to 38 a month to help lift cash flow. The U.S. Federal Aviation Administration had capped output of the narrowbody plane in January after a door plug blew out from a 737 Max 9 flown by Alaska Airlines.

Boeing (BA) plans to resume production of its 767, 777 and 777X planes in Everett, Washington, "in the days ahead," according to a statement. Production of the 787 in South Carolina wasn't halted by the strike.

data.title: Super Micro Computer sinks even as CEO 'confident' stock will not be delisted
data.description:
• Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) shares fell more than 8% on Tuesday, even as CEO Charles Liang told Reuters he was "confident" shares of the artificial intelligence server maker will not be delisted from the Nasdaq.
  • On Friday, Super Micro received a letter from the Nasdaq informing the company that the exchange granted its request for an extension. The extension gives the company until February 25, 2025, to file its fiscal 2024 Annual Report on Form 10-K, its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the period ended Sept. 30, 2024 and any other required filings.
  • As such, common stock in Super Micro will remain listed on the exchange during the exception period. The company currently expects to file all the required reports by February 25, 2025.
  • Earlier this month, an independent special committee appointed by Super Micro said it found no evidence of misconduct by the company's board of directors or audit committee. However, as part of the investigating committee’s recommendations, Super Micro will appoint a new CFO, chief compliance officer and general counsel. The company named Kenneth Cheung, previously the vice president of finance, as its new chief accounting officer.

(Seeking Alpha's Tiyashi Datta and Preeti Singh contributed to this story.)

data.title: Union chief predicts US Steel's long-term decline if Japanese take over
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) -2.5% in Tuesday's trading as United Steelworkers union head David McCall said Tuesday he has not received assurances that Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) is committed to ensuring the lasting success of the American firm in its proposed takeover.

One of McCall's biggest concerns is that Nippon (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) may import steel into the U.S. from its international mills, a move he said would erode the iconic U.S. company.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) wants a return on its investment, "and I understand that but it can't be harvesting our facilities and letting them slowly but surely over a period of time deteriorate so that they can then bring product in from their other facilities around the world and have access to our market," McCall told Reuters in an interview.

The Japanese company has denied it would import steel from its international mills into U.S. and said it aims to become an "insider" in the U.S., a market it considers crucial to its growth plans.

McCall also criticized Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) for not trying to stop or intervene in "bullying" from US Steel (X) CEO David Burritt to close the deal.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) unveiled details of the various commitments it has made to win union support, including on job security and investments in facilities.
